title: Bytes, Text I/O, Encodings, Newlines, and Cleanup · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed maintenance review reconstructs bytes, text i/o, encodings, newlines, and cleanup from repository state, input bytes, decoded text, tokens, counters, ranking keys, report records, stdout, stderr, exit status, tests, package artifact, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for explicit text-I/O boundary after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish raw bytes, decoded str values, code points, grapheme clusters, and serialized output at every boundary.
Choose and document UTF-8 or another declared encoding instead of relying on locale-dependent defaults.
Select strict rejection, replacement, ignore, or surrogate handling from the stakeholder evidence duty and expose data loss.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Bytes Text Boundary · changed case · Review Bytes Text Boundary
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Bookbot team handles case 4494 during a digital-humanities corpus revision. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Build deterministic Python evidence to decode bytes under an explicit UTF-8 and cleanup contract without hiding malformed input; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use context management and narrow exception scopes so handles close and the causal error remains observable.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Context Cleanup.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use context management and narrow exception scopes so handles close and the causal error remains observable.
